steps.title: Use the area formula
steps.content: The area of a rectangle is length times width.

steps.title: Substitute the dimensions
steps.content: With length $3$ and width $39$, the area expression is $3\cdot39$.

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content: The expression $3\cdot39$ matches Choice D — $3·39$. Choice A — $2\left(3+39\right)$ is perimeter, Choice B — $2\left(3·39\right)$ doubles the area, and Choice C — $3+39$ only adds the side lengths.
